banner: audited origin/main; working HEAD d6064dd2 was 134 behind / 10 ahead on feat/realtime-datapoint-clock
finding: GREEN held + strengthened since A6(@51e3f5b0). 9 hub commits since A6, every security-relevant change is a hardening; mira-web touched once (UI label/FAB, #1944). Zero new stranger-reachable surface.
middleware.ts allowlist UNCHANGED since A6 (empty diff) -> no new pre-auth attack surface; global edge JWE gate intact.

6 api routes changed, 0 new route.ts added (only added api file is admin/review/queue/__tests__/route.test.ts). Every changed route still sits behind sessionOr401(); admin routes now behind requireCapability().

New RBAC primitive lib/capabilities.ts = single source of truth for authz, fed to BOTH nav (/api/me getCapabilities) and API guards (requireCapability). Each cap maps to the EXACT pre-existing signal (review_queue.*<-isReviewAdmin(email); platform.users.read<-status=='admin'; workspace.*<-any authed tenant user). Does NOT widen access; kills a nav-vs-API drift (sidebar hardcoded role=admin). Server-only; unit-tested (capabilities.test.ts).

api/assets/[id]/chat now passes allowTenantFallback:false to retrieveManualChunks -> grounded answers cite only manufacturer-scoped manuals, no within-tenant cross-vendor fallback. Helper remains tenant-scoped by ctx.tenantId in all branches (default fallback is within-tenant, NOT cross-tenant), so this is citation-precision hardening, not a leak fix.
evidence: assets/[id]/chat/route.ts:+allowTenantFallback:false; manual-rag.ts:152 default ??true, all queries bound to ctx.tenantId

finding: ingest-v2 (memory-bounded streaming) implemented in node-knowledge-ingest.ts (not a new route). knowledge_entries INSERT pins is_private = true as a LITERAL (L168), preserving #1903 tenant privacy under hybrid read filter (is_private=false OR tenant_id=caller). No-DB vitest regression guard asserts the literal + params never contain false.

platform-accounts 'safer account actions' = UX/a11y only (window.confirm before Revoke/Expire, per-row aria-labels, search-aware count, error toast). account-actions.ts is JSX-free pure helpers, no mutation/authz. Mutation route still gated platform.users.read (status=='admin'); nav label 'Accounts'->'Platform accounts', capability unchanged. 'No account or seed data changed.'
evidence: e5f2ddc2; account-actions.ts (helpers only); access-control.ts label-only diff

finding: Upload caps centralized to MAX_UPLOAD_BYTES (config.ts, default 50MB via NEXT_PUBLIC_MAX_UPLOAD_MB, documented for 31-33MB OEM manuals). Bounded + server-enforced on every upload route; ingest-v2 is memory-bounded streaming -> no trivial large-file memory DoS. Old 20/10MB hardcodes removed.
evidence: config.ts:30-31; uploads/route.ts + namespace/node/[id]/files/route.ts read the constant

finding: No hardcoded api-key/secret/password/token assignment literals (>=16 chars) in mira-hub/src or mira-web/src on origin/main (tests/examples excluded). Clean.
evidence: git grep -nIE assignment-literal on origin/main = empty

finding: CARRY-FWD: quickstart/ask rate limiter still in-memory/single-instance (unchanged since A6, route not in changed set). Fine for single-VPS beta; port to shared store before horizontal scale.

NEW hygiene note: capabilities.ts is server-only (imports node fs transitively via review-queue) with an explicit 'client must read /api/me' header. A stray client-component import would pull fs into the bundle = build-time failure, not a runtime leak. No guardrail enforces it beyond the comment.

God-nodes (kg_query insights): sessionOr401() deg95 + withTenantContext() deg80 remain the 2 highest-degree HUB nodes (engine.py 99 / Supervisor 92 are engine). The new requireCapability() guard composes AFTER sessionOr401 on every admin route -> authz still funnels through the same most-depended-on primitives, not a parallel bolt-on.

KG staleness: graph.json built_at_commit=308e7b55 (~Jun 8), predates #1932 -> capabilities.ts/requireCapability ABSENT (0 grep matches). Orphans = only CI/compose/__init__ nodes; ZERO unguarded API routes orphaned (matches A6). Appended hand-extracted triples for the new authz surface (a7-route-auth.jsonl) per the no-rebuild fallback.
